Bash(/tmp/test_cascade.md << 'EOF'
# CSS Cascade Analysis for JCSM Project

## Load Order on Public Pages \(e.g., index.html\)
1. Tailwind CDN \(script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"\) - generates @layer utilities
2. styles.css \(linked AFTER Tailwind\) - custom unlayered styles

## Critical Finding: Tailwind @layer Structure
Tailwind CDN uses @layer directives to organize:
- @layer base { ... }    /* Reset/defaults */
- @layer components { ... } /* Component classes */
- @layer utilities { ... }   /* Utility classes like .text-gray-500 */

## CSS Cascade Rules \(MDN Standard\)

Per CSS Cascade specification, when two rules have the same specificity:
**Source order wins** - the rule that appears LATER in the stylesheet takes precedence

However, @layer rules MUST be re-ordered by the cascade, regardless of source order:
- @layer base
- @layer components  
- @layer utilities
- \(unlayered styles\)

## Critical Question: Do Unlayered > Layered?

According to CSS Cascade Level 3 and Tailwind's own documentation:
"Styles defined outside of a @layer are treated as if they were added to the top of the stylesheet"

This is INCORRECT for the CSS Cascade. Actually:
- Unlayered declarations beat layered ones at the SAME specificity level
- This is because unlayered is treated as having higher cascade priority than any @layer

Reference: https://www.w3.org/TR/css-cascade-3/#cascade-origin

EOF)

def translate_file\(filename, replacements\):
    with open\(filename, 'r'\) as f:
        content = f.read\(\)
    count = 0
    for old, new in replacements:
        if old in content:
            content = content.replace\(old, new\)
            count += 1
    with open\(filename, 'w'\) as f:
        f.write\(content\)
    print\(f"  {filename}: {count}/{len\(replacements\)} applied"\)
